module: FSM Modules 9+10 — Repair Domain + Template Engine

RepairOrder.purpose: Central repair lifecycle aggregate. Tracks diagnosis, tasks, parts, checklists, resolution.

RepairDiagnosis.purpose: Symptom analysis, root cause, specialist/parts/quote flags for a RepairOrder.

RepairTask.purpose: Individual work items within a RepairOrder, sequenced and assigned.

RepairAction.purpose: Timestamped action log entries within a RepairOrder.

RepairPartUsage.purpose: Parts reserved or consumed during a repair, with inventory linkage hooks.

RepairChecklist.purpose: Checklist execution context within a RepairOrder.

RepairResolution.purpose: Structured resolution record capturing root cause, preventive action, and verification.

RepairTemplate.purpose: Reusable repair procedure template for automatic task/parts/checklist generation.

RepairTemplateStep.purpose: Ordered step within a RepairTemplate, generates RepairTask on instantiation.

RepairTemplatePart.purpose: Part specification within a RepairTemplate, generates RepairPartUsage on instantiation.

RepairTemplateChecklist.purpose: Checklist specification within a RepairTemplate, generates RepairChecklist on instantiation.

ServiceJob.reason: Service jobs can trigger repair orders; existing warranty_claim_id and is_warranty_job fields already support the workflow.

Premises.reason: Premises requires spatial repair intelligence for risk assessment and history queries.

SchedulingSurfaceProvider.added: RepairOrder added to ENTITY_TYPES. getEventsForRange, getEventsForUser, getEventsForPremises, getEventsForTeam all include RepairOrders via repairsForRange() private helper.
SchedulingSurfaceProvider.reason: RepairOrder implements SchedulableEntity; repairs must appear on the unified dispatch board and calendar.

duplicates_detected.ServiceJob: ServiceJob already captures repair-like workflows (is_warranty_job, warranty_claim_id). RepairOrder extends this capability with a dedicated lifecycle rather than replacing it. ServiceJob.repairOrders() bridges the two.
duplicates_detected.ChecklistRun: RepairChecklist is repair-scoped and simpler (no polymorphic runnable). ChecklistRun remains the canonical execution engine for complex, template-driven checklists attached to ServiceJobs/InspectionInstances.
duplicates_detected.WarrantyClaim: No duplication — RepairOrder references WarrantyClaim directly by FK. Warranty domain remains canonical.
duplicates_detected.asset_service_events: The site_assets table already had an 'event_type' comment including 'repair' in migration 2026_04_02_000600. This is a log table and does not conflict with RepairOrder as an aggregate.

future_merge_candidates.ChecklistRun_RepairChecklist: When checklist complexity grows, RepairChecklist could be superseded by a ChecklistRun with runnable_type=RepairOrder. Defer until needed.
future_merge_candidates.RepairPartUsage_Inventory: RepairPartUsage has stock_location and movement_reference hooks ready for full inventory module integration when that module is available.
future_merge_candidates.ServiceRepairBridge: RepairOrder.originatingServiceJob() / ServiceJob.repairOrders() bridge is live. Future: add RepairOrder as a runnable_type to ChecklistRun for unified checklist execution.
future_merge_candidates.CRMSignals: All CRM repair signals (CrmRepairDetected, CrmReplacementCandidate, CrmAgreementCandidate, CrmRecurringCandidate) are defined and registered but have empty listener arrays pending CRM pipeline integration pass.
future_merge_candidates.FinanceSignals: RepairInvoiceGenerated and RepairClaimOffsetApplied are defined and registered but have empty listeners pending Finance domain pass.
future_merge_candidates.SchedulingSignals: RepairScheduled, RepairRescheduled, RepairDispatchAssigned are defined and registered but have empty listeners pending Dispatch pass.
